interviewStages: Behavioral: customer focus, ownership, working with sales domain
codingTopics: OO design: model a business domain such as workflows with triggers and actions
Multi-tenant data structures: per-tenant rate limiting, isolation
Streaming: rolling aggregates over tenant events
Graph and DAG: workflow evaluation, dependency resolution
Search and filters: AND/OR queries over records
systemDesignTopics: Design an email send pipeline with deliverability optimization
Design AI conversation intelligence on calls

coding: At Salesloft, model a workflow engine with triggers, conditions, and actions. Evaluate a workflow DAG and detect cycles.
Implement a per-tenant rate limiter so one customer cannot exhaust shared capacity. Discuss the token-bucket design.
Given a stream of tenant events, maintain rolling per-entity scores with bounded memory.
Implement a filter query over records supporting AND/OR on custom fields. Discuss indexing for a multi-tenant store.
Deduplicate records via fuzzy clustering on name and email. Discuss the matching and complexity.
behavioral: Tell me about a project you are proud of at Salesloft scale and the hardest tradeoff you made.
Give me an example that shows customer focus, ownership, working with sales domain.
Describe a time you disagreed with a teammate or manager. How did it resolve?
Tell me about a time you took ownership of an ambiguous problem end to end.
systemDesign: Design an email send pipeline with deliverability optimization.
Design AI conversation intelligence on calls.
values: Salesloft succeeds when its customers do. Describe a time you solved for the customer even at a cost to the team.
Tell me about a time you handled a tricky multi-tenant isolation or permissions problem.
How do you balance enterprise customization against product simplicity?
other: How does Salesloft compare to Outreach?

Salesloft Interview Guide (2026): Sales Engagement Platform

				techinterview.org

- What they actually ask

Updated May 5, 2026 · techinterview.org

⏱ 1 min read

Salesloft
salesloft.com ↗

Salesloft is the close competitor to Outreach in sales engagement — used by 5000+ companies. The interview emphasizes email/call sequencing, CRM integration, and AI-augmented sales workflows.

Process

Recruiter screen → 60-minute coding phone (DSA medium) → onsite virtual: 2 coding, 1 system design, 1 craft deep-dive, 1 behavioral. Cycle: 3–4 weeks.

What they actually ask

- Design an email send pipeline with deliverability optimization

- Design CRM bi-directional sync (Salesforce, HubSpot)

- Design AI conversation intelligence on calls

- Coding: medium DSA, often with workflow framing

- Behavioral: customer focus, ownership, working with sales domain

Levels and comp (2026)

- SE II: $140K–$175K total

- Senior SE: $205K–$270K

- Staff: $295K–$385K

- Principal: $410K–$540K

Prep priorities

- Be fluent in Ruby on Rails (the bulk of the codebase)

- Understand email deliverability and CRM data models

- Brush up on call analytics and ML for conversation insights

Frequently Asked Questions

Is Salesloft remote-friendly?

Hybrid in Atlanta (HQ), San Francisco. Many engineering roles fully remote within US.

How does Salesloft compare to Outreach?

Outreach has slightly larger market share. Salesloft is closer in features. Comp is comparable.

What is the engineering culture?

Mature SaaS, customer-driven, calm pace. Strong work-life balance.
